threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know someone just moved here when they ask for a takeaway on a weekday at 6 PM and are shocked by the wait time.
Last night on the 3 bus, a man serenaded the driver with a classic soul tune. Only in Brixton would you see a bus turning into a stage.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a kid on the street calling their grandmother ‘Oma’ in Arabic while the smell of kebab wafted through the air.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a bar where they serve beer in dirty old glass jars, and it's somehow the hipster haven everyone loves.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they still call Dolores Park 'Turtle Hill' and actually know where to find the best pupusas at 24th and South Van Ness.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 14 Mission reciting his poetry to a captive audience. Muni really is the stage for our city's stories, raw and unfiltered.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always miss the small, tucked-away bars that serve the best vermut. They flock to the busy spots, but the hidden ones hold the real charm.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their reluctance to use 'adeu' when saying goodbye; it's always a 'bye' or a wave instead.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the London Overground' instead of just 'the Overground.' Everyone else here knows it’s just part of life, not a tourist attraction.
Caught a glimpse of a bloke serenading his mates with a tinny on the 35 last night. Only in Brixton would that feel like a normal Tuesday.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Spotted an old lady in a bright orange tracksuit walking her ferret on a leash by the Arminiusmarkthalle. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can grab a late-night döner while watching a spontaneous techno party erupt on the street. Only in Berlin do late-night snacks come with a side of bass.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their nonchalance when the Muni is late, while the newbies are pacing like it's the end of the world.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy at the Powell station juggling while waiting for the N-Judah. Just another day on Muni, merging art with transit chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Parc Güell but miss out on the real vibrancy of Gràcia's local plazas, where the true essence of the neighborhood thrives.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll often see them staring at their phones in the middle of the street, trying to navigate while missing the beauty around them.
